description: Self-improving error pattern database. Agents should append new entries when encountering novel error patterns.

categories.leakage: Any form of data leakage detection
categories.gate: Gate script execution and validation errors

entries.symptom: Gate script reports wrong error code when JSON parse fails for feature engineering report
entries.root_cause: except block used feature_group_spec_missing_or_invalid instead of feature_engineering_report_invalid
entries.fix: Change error code to feature_engineering_report_invalid in the except block
entries.prevention: Always match error codes to the specific artifact being parsed, not a related artifact

entries.symptom: transport_drop_ci shows [0.0, 0.0] CI bounds implying bootstrapped CI when none was computed
entries.root_cause: Hard-coded [0.0, 0.0] for ci_95 and 0.0 for ci_width in transport drop block
entries.fix: Replace with null and add ci_note: not_computed_point_estimate_only
entries.prevention: Never hard-code placeholder CI values; use null with explicit note when CI is not computed

entries.symptom: to_float() accepts inf and nan as valid float values, causing downstream gates to process non-finite metrics
entries.root_cause: Missing math.isfinite guard after float conversion
entries.fix: Add math.isfinite(v) check after float() conversion; return None if not finite
entries.prevention: All to_float implementations must include isfinite guard. Template: if not math.isfinite(v): return None

entries.symptom: Gate passes in strict mode despite having warnings, because finish() only checks failures
entries.root_cause: should_fail = bool(failures) ignores warnings in strict mode
entries.fix: Change to: should_fail = bool(failures) or (args.strict and bool(warnings))
entries.prevention: All gate finish() must use the pattern: bool(failures) or (args.strict and bool(warnings))

entries.symptom: Feature stability frequency analysis uses same random_state across all repeats
entries.root_cause: random_state=seed in loop does not change with repeat_idx
entries.fix: Use random_state=seed + repeat_idx to vary state per repeat
entries.prevention: In any loop performing repeated random sampling, ensure random_state varies with iteration index

entries.symptom: Model selection fails with candidate_pool_too_small when fewer than 3 models succeed
entries.root_cause: Insufficient model families selected, or optional backends not installed
entries.fix: Increase model families in --model-pool, raise --max-trials-per-family, or install optional backends (xgboost, catboost)
entries.prevention: For small datasets, use at least balanced preset (10 families). Check env_doctor for backend availability.

entries.symptom: ValueError: Cannot convert NaN to integer when assigning NaN to integer-typed numpy array
entries.root_cause: Attempting to assign np.nan to an integer-dtype column or array
entries.fix: Use DataFrame.loc[mask, col] = np.nan which auto-promotes dtype, or cast column to float first
entries.prevention: Before assigning NaN, ensure target column is float-compatible. Use pd.array with nullable integer dtype if needed.

entries.symptom: Training exceeds 20 minutes and times out with large dataset + many model candidates
entries.root_cause: Large dataset (>5000 rows) combined with comprehensive model pool and full bootstrap
entries.fix: Reduce model families, lower --max-trials-per-family, use conservative preset, or set --n-jobs to utilize multi-core
entries.prevention: For datasets >5000 rows: use adaptive bootstrap (auto-enabled), limit to balanced preset, set --n-jobs min(4, cpu_count)

entries.symptom: Warning: significant prevalence shift between train/valid/test splits
entries.root_cause: Random or temporal splitting created unbalanced prevalence across splits
entries.fix: Use stratified splitting (--strategy stratified_grouped) or verify temporal split preserves approximate prevalence
entries.prevention: Always check event rates per split after splitting. Use split_data.py which auto-warns on prevalence shift.

entries.symptom: Execution attestation gate fails because key files do not exist
entries.root_cause: Keypair not generated before running attestation gate
entries.fix: Run generate_execution_attestation.py which auto-bootstraps keypair if missing, or manually create with openssl
entries.prevention: Use mlgg onboarding flow which handles key generation in step 6

entries.symptom: Pipeline fails requiring --compare-manifest but no baseline manifest exists
entries.root_cause: First run has no baseline to compare against
entries.fix: Use --allow-missing-compare for first-run bootstrap only
entries.prevention: After first successful run, always save manifest as baseline for subsequent runs

entries.symptom: evaluation_quality_gate fails because evaluation_metric_path leaf does not match primary_metric
entries.root_cause: Request JSON declares primary_metric as one name but evaluation_metric_path points to a different metric
entries.fix: Ensure evaluation_metric_path terminal token matches primary_metric after normalization (e.g., pr_auc matches pr_auc)
entries.prevention: Use consistent metric naming. For publication-grade, primary_metric must be pr_auc.

entries.symptom: Covariate shift gate warns/fails due to high train-vs-test separability (AUC > 0.7)
entries.root_cause: Significant distribution drift between training and holdout data
entries.fix: Investigate feature distributions across splits; consider temporal re-splitting or feature removal for highly drifting features
entries.prevention: Use covariate_shift_gate.py early in pipeline. For temporal data, ensure splits respect temporal ordering.

entries.symptom: External validation gate fails because transport drop exceeds threshold
entries.root_cause: Model performance degrades significantly on external cohort compared to internal test
entries.fix: Investigate domain differences; consider domain adaptation, feature harmonization, or recalibration
entries.prevention: Include external cohort early in development cycle. Check distribution overlap before expecting good transport.

entries.symptom: Calibration gate fails because ECE exceeds threshold on internal or external cohort
entries.root_cause: Probability calibration not applied or inadequate for the model family
entries.fix: Enable calibration in training (--calibration-method isotonic or platt). For small datasets use platt.
entries.prevention: Always enable calibration. Check calibration plots. For tree-based models, isotonic often works better.

entries.symptom: Journal reviewers flag missing subgroup equity analysis; fairness metrics computed but never validated by any gate
entries.root_cause: Fairness metrics (equalized_odds_gap, disparate_impact_ratio) were computed in train_select_evaluate.py but no gate validated them against thresholds
entries.fix: Added fairness_equity_gate.py (gate #30) that validates equalized odds gap (<0.15), disparate impact ratio (>0.80/four-fifths rule), and per-subgroup PR-AUC minimums
entries.prevention: Any metric computed in training pipeline should have a corresponding gate that validates it against fail-closed thresholds

entries.symptom: Small dataset models pass all gates despite EPV < 10; reviewers ask for sample size justification
entries.root_cause: EPV was computed and reported but never hard-gated. Riley et al. 2019/2025 criteria not enforced
entries.fix: Added sample_size_gate.py that enforces EPV >= 10 (fail), >= 20 (warn), min 100 events, shrinkage factor >= 0.9
entries.prevention: All quantitative adequacy metrics should have corresponding fail-closed gates, not just informational reporting

entries.symptom: TRIPOD+AI 2024 has 27 items but gate only validated 11; reviewers reject for incomplete adherence
entries.root_cause: reporting_bias_gate.py only included original subset of TRIPOD+AI items, missing 2024 additions
entries.fix: Added 6 new required items: study_design_and_data_source_described, outcome_prevalence_or_event_rate_reported, calibration_metrics_reported, fairness_assessment_reported, model_availability_or_code_sharing_reported, data_availability_statement_provided
entries.prevention: Track guideline version updates (TRIPOD+AI BMJ 2024;385:e078378) and update gate validation items accordingly

entries.symptom: Top journals (JAMA, Lancet) require IDI alongside NRI for model comparison; only NRI was available
entries.root_cause: Integrated Discrimination Improvement (Pencina et al. 2008) not implemented despite NRI being present
entries.fix: Added _integrated_discrimination_improvement() function computing IDI, discrimination slope, and relative IDI vs both prevalence and logistic baselines
entries.prevention: When implementing one model comparison metric (NRI), also implement its companion metrics (IDI, DeLong) for completeness

entries.symptom: Nature Medicine and Lancet DH strongly recommend SHAP-based feature explanations; only permutation importance available
entries.root_cause: SHAP library not integrated; only model-agnostic permutation importance implemented
entries.fix: Not yet resolved. SHAP is optional dependency. Consider adding optional SHAP computation when shap package is installed.
entries.prevention: Track journal requirements for interpretability. SHAP is becoming standard for tree-based and linear models.
entries.planned_fix: Add optional shap_explainability_gate as non-blocking INFO gate when shap package is installed; compute TreeExplainer for tree-based models, LinearExplainer for linear models, and KernelExplainer as fallback

entries.symptom: Reviewers ask whether missingness is MCAR/MAR/MNAR; framework validates policy but not mechanism
entries.root_cause: missingness_policy_gate enforces imputation strategy compliance but does not test the missing data mechanism (Little's MCAR test)
entries.fix: Not yet resolved. Consider adding optional Little's MCAR test and MNAR sensitivity analysis.
entries.prevention: Distinguish between policy enforcement (is the right method used?) and mechanism validation (is the assumption justified?)
entries.planned_fix: Add Little's MCAR test as optional check in missingness_policy_gate via --mcar-test flag; add MNAR sensitivity analysis via --sensitivity-analysis flag using pattern-mixture models

entries.symptom: PROBAST+AI was updated in BMJ March 2025 with expanded signalling questions; current gate uses 2019 version
entries.root_cause: PROBAST+AI update (Moons et al. BMJ 2025) expanded from original to 16 development + 18 evaluation signalling questions
entries.fix: Not yet resolved. Current 6-item PROBAST check covers core domains but should be expanded to match 2025 signalling questions.
entries.prevention: Monitor BMJ/EQUATOR Network for guideline updates annually
entries.planned_fix: Update reporting_bias_gate.py to implement full 22-item PROBAST+AI-2025 checklist (16 development + 18 evaluation signalling questions per Moons et al. BMJ 2025) replacing the current 6-item PROBAST-2019 core domain check

entries.symptom: JAMA Network Open 2025 showed ICD codes can leak same-admission outcomes (label leakage)
entries.root_cause: Diagnostic codes assigned during same hospitalization can encode the outcome being predicted
entries.fix: definition_variable_guard.py already blocks disease-defining variables; feature_lineage_gate.py blocks lineage leakage. But same-admission ICD code leakage is a subtler pattern that requires temporal awareness.
entries.prevention: When using ICD/diagnosis codes as predictors, verify they were assigned BEFORE the prediction time point (t_index), not during the same encounter.

entries.symptom: batch_journal_review.py fails with JSON parse error or missing required fields in manifest
entries.root_cause: Batch manifest file does not conform to batch_manifest.v1 contract (missing 'projects' array or invalid project entries)
entries.fix: Validate manifest against references/batch-manifest.example.json template. Each project entry requires 'id' and 'path' fields.
entries.prevention: Use batch-manifest.example.json as template. Validate JSON before running batch review.

entries.symptom: Batch review reports 'Project directory not found' for one or more projects in manifest
entries.root_cause: Project path in manifest does not exist on disk or is not accessible
entries.fix: Verify all project paths in manifest exist and are absolute paths. Use expanduser() for ~ paths.
entries.prevention: Validate project paths before running batch review. Use absolute paths in manifest.

entries.symptom: split_protocol_gate reports entity_overlap_count > 0, indicating the same patient ID appears in multiple splits
entries.root_cause: Data was split by row rather than by patient (entity), causing the same patient to appear in both train and test sets
entries.fix: Re-run split_data.py with --entity-col <patient_id_column> to enforce entity-level splitting. Delete the contaminated split files and regenerate.
entries.prevention: Always specify --entity-col when patients have multiple rows. split_data.py defaults to entity-level splitting when entity column is provided.

entries.symptom: leakage_gate reports row_overlap_count > 0 between train and test CSVs
entries.root_cause: Identical rows (same feature values and label) appear in both training and test sets, directly contaminating the holdout evaluation
entries.fix: Re-run split_data.py to regenerate splits from the original unsplit dataset. Never manually copy or move rows between split files.
entries.prevention: Never edit split CSV files manually. Always regenerate splits from the source dataset using split_data.py. Run leakage_gate immediately after any split operation.

entries.symptom: clinical_metrics_gate fails because evaluation_report.json lacks the subgroup_performance section
entries.root_cause: train_select_evaluate.py was run without --subgroup-cols flag, so no subgroup breakdowns were computed and stored in the evaluation report
entries.fix: Re-run train_select_evaluate.py with --subgroup-cols <col1,col2>. Minimum clinically required columns: age_group and sex/gender when available in the dataset.
entries.prevention: Always specify at least one clinically relevant subgroup column. Define subgroup_columns in request.json and ensure train_select_evaluate.py reads it.

entries.symptom: publication_gate blocks the publication-ready declaration because one or more upstream gates have status != pass
entries.root_cause: publication_gate is a fail-closed aggregator: it issues a publication-ready attestation only when ALL 31 gates have passed. Any single gate failure propagates to block publication.
entries.fix: Read gate_results_summary.json to identify failed gates. Fix each failure in order of severity (CRITICAL first). Re-run the full pipeline until all 31 gates show status=pass.
entries.prevention: Run gates in the canonical pipeline order via mlgg.py workflow. Never skip gates. Publication declaration is only valid when all 31 gates pass in the same pipeline run.

entries.symptom: self_critique_gate issues a warning or fails because the aggregate quality_score falls below the configured threshold (default 0.7)
entries.root_cause: Self-critique computes a weighted aggregate across all gate reports. A sub-threshold score indicates systemic quality deficits across multiple dimensions, beyond any single gate failure.
entries.fix: Review self_critique_report.json for the lowest-scoring dimensions. Prioritize fixing the top 3 dimension deficits as they have the highest aggregate impact. Re-run affected gates after remediation.
entries.prevention: Run self_critique_gate after all other gates pass as a final pre-submission quality check. Use its dimension scores as a checklist for top-journal readiness.

entries.symptom: security_audit_gate reports HMAC signature mismatch for a model artifact, indicating the file was modified after signing
entries.root_cause: The model file was overwritten, re-serialized, or corrupted after the HMAC signature was computed and stored in the attestation record
entries.fix: If modification was intentional (re-training): re-sign the new artifact using generate_execution_attestation.py --re-sign. If accidental: restore the originally signed version from backup.
entries.prevention: Treat signed model artifacts as immutable. Never overwrite a signed model file. If re-training is needed, increment the model version and create a new signed artifact.

entries.symptom: manifest_lock gate fails because the SHA-256 hash of one or more evidence artifacts has changed since the manifest was sealed
entries.root_cause: An evidence file (gate report JSON, model artifact, or evaluation output) was modified after the manifest was sealed, breaking the provenance chain
entries.fix: Identify which artifact changed using the manifest diff in the gate report. If the change was intentional, re-run the affected gate and re-seal the manifest. If accidental, restore from the sealed artifact.
entries.prevention: Seal the manifest as the absolute final step of the pipeline run. Do not modify any gate report files after pipeline completion. Use --allow-update only during active iterative development.

entries.symptom: tuning_leakage_gate detects that hyperparameter optimization used the held-out validation or test set directly or indirectly
entries.root_cause: Hyperparameter search used test set performance as the optimization objective, or early stopping was monitored on the test split, creating indirect leakage into model selection
entries.fix: Ensure all hyperparameter tuning uses cross-validation on the training set only. Never pass validation_data pointing to val/test split into any tuning loop. Verify with tuning_leakage_gate.py --check-cv-strategy.
entries.prevention: Use nested cross-validation for hyperparameter tuning. The test set must be touched exactly once for final evaluation. train_select_evaluate.py enforces this separation by design.

entries.symptom: model_selection_audit_gate fails because fewer than 3 candidate models with valid CV scores exist, making one-SE rule application statistically invalid
entries.root_cause: Model training produced fewer than 3 successful candidates (others may have failed, timed out, or been filtered). The one-SE rule requires a sufficient candidate pool to estimate standard error meaningfully.
entries.fix: Increase --model-pool size, raise --max-trials-per-family, or install optional backends (xgboost, catboost, lightgbm). A minimum of 3 candidates is required; 5+ is recommended for robust one-SE rule application.
entries.prevention: Use the balanced or comprehensive preset to ensure at least 5+ model families. Run mlgg.py doctor to check available backends before starting training.

entries.symptom: permutation_significance_gate reports p_value > 0.05, indicating model performance is not statistically distinguishable from random label permutation
entries.root_cause: The model fails to capture meaningful signal, typically caused by: insufficient sample size (low statistical power), severe class imbalance, or genuinely non-predictive features in the feature set
entries.fix: Check sample_size_gate output for EPV adequacy. If sample size is adequate, review feature quality and relevance. A non-significant result is a valid scientific finding — do not increase permutation iterations to force significance.
entries.prevention: Run sample_size_gate before training to confirm adequate statistical power. A non-significant permutation test should be reported as a finding, not suppressed.

entries.symptom: ci_matrix_gate fails because pr_auc_ci_95, roc_auc_ci_95, or other required CI bounds are missing, null, or [NaN, NaN] in the evaluation report
entries.root_cause: Bootstrap CI computation failed silently (too few bootstrap samples, all samples identical, or numerical instability) and the result was stored as null/NaN instead of raising an error
entries.fix: Increase --bootstrap-iterations (minimum 1000 for publication). Check for degenerate bootstrap samples (all predictions identical). Ensure eval set has at least 30 positive events for stable CI estimation.
entries.prevention: Apply the isfinite guard to all CI values before writing evaluation_report.json. Fail loudly if CI computation yields NaN instead of silently storing it.

entries.symptom: imbalance_policy_gate fails because the actual imbalance handling strategy used in training does not match the strategy declared in request.json
entries.root_cause: request.json specifies an imbalance strategy (e.g., class_weight_balanced) but training was run with a different or no strategy, creating a contract violation between declared methodology and actual execution
entries.fix: Ensure --imbalance-strategy in train_select_evaluate.py matches the imbalance_strategy field in request.json. Re-run training with the declared strategy.
entries.prevention: train_select_evaluate.py should read imbalance_strategy directly from request.json rather than accepting it as a separate CLI argument, eliminating the mismatch risk.

entries.symptom: prediction_replay_gate fails because re-running inference with the saved model on the saved test features produces predictions that differ from the stored prediction_report.json
entries.root_cause: Non-deterministic model inference (unseeded randomness in predict() path), or the stored model artifact was regenerated/modified after predictions were recorded
entries.fix: Ensure model artifact is the exact file used for the original predictions. Set all random seeds before inference. For stochastic models (e.g., MC dropout), disable dropout at inference time.
entries.prevention: Always call np.random.seed(seed) and torch.manual_seed(seed) / tf.random.set_seed(seed) before model.predict(). Treat prediction CSVs and model artifacts as an inseparable pair.

entries.symptom: calibration_dca_gate fails because the calibration curve is non-monotonic or HL-test p < 0.05, indicating systematic miscalibration
entries.root_cause: Model output probabilities are uncalibrated (e.g., raw SVM scores, tree ensemble without Platt scaling), causing systematic over- or under-confidence in predicted probabilities
entries.fix: Apply post-hoc calibration: CalibratedClassifierCV(cv='prefit', method='isotonic') or Platt scaling. Re-run calibration_dca_gate after calibration.
entries.prevention: Always wrap final model in CalibratedClassifierCV before writing to artifact. Treat calibration as a required step, not optional, for any clinical deployment.

entries.symptom: covariate_shift_gate reports KS p-value < 0.05 for one or more features, indicating train/test distribution mismatch
entries.root_cause: Features have meaningfully different distributions between training and test sets, often caused by: temporal shift, site-specific collection protocols, or non-random split strategy
entries.fix: Investigate the flagged features for distribution changes. Use stratified splits by relevant covariates. If shift is expected (temporal deployment), document it as an applicability caveat in TRIPOD+AI Item 4.
entries.prevention: Run covariate_shift_gate immediately after split_data.py. Temporal splits are inherently shift-prone — always report this as a study limitation.

entries.symptom: definition_variable_guard fails because one or more features in the model are definition variables that directly encode the outcome by construction
entries.root_cause: A feature is functionally equivalent to the outcome (e.g., 'hospitalized_for_X' used to predict 'diagnosed_with_X'), creating trivial leakage via outcome definition
entries.fix: Remove all definition variables from the feature set. Review feature_lineage.json to identify outcome-proximate features. Use domain expertise to distinguish predictors from post-outcome variables.
entries.prevention: Apply definition_variable_guard as the first gate after feature engineering. Maintain a blocked-features list in request.json for any feature that is definitionally linked to the outcome.

entries.symptom: distribution_generalization_gate reports transport_drop (AUC_source - AUC_target) > threshold, indicating model performance degrades unacceptably across sites/populations
entries.root_cause: Model relies on site-specific feature distributions that do not transfer to the target deployment population, causing performance degradation when applied outside the development cohort
entries.fix: Investigate features driving the transport drop using feature importance comparison between source and target. Consider domain adaptation, recalibration, or restricting the model's applicability scope in TRIPOD+AI Item 23.
entries.prevention: Train on multi-site data when possible. Document deployment context clearly. Run distribution_generalization_gate on any new deployment site before clinical use.

entries.symptom: evaluation_quality_gate reports composite quality score < threshold (typically 0.7), indicating insufficient evaluation rigor for publication
entries.root_cause: Evaluation report is missing required fields (CI bounds, calibration metrics, subgroup performance, DCA), uses incompatible metrics (e.g., accuracy-only for imbalanced data), or CI computation failed
entries.fix: Regenerate evaluation report with all required fields. Ensure --bootstrap-iterations >= 1000, --include-calibration, and --include-dca flags are set in train_select_evaluate.py.
entries.prevention: Use train_select_evaluate.py default configuration which includes all required evaluation components. Do not override --skip-calibration or --skip-dca in production runs.

entries.symptom: execution_attestation_gate fails HMAC verification — the pipeline execution receipt was tampered with or signed with a different key
entries.root_cause: The HMAC-SHA256 signature on the execution receipt does not match the payload, indicating either: (1) the receipt was modified post-signing, (2) a different MLGG_HMAC_SECRET was used for signing vs. verification, or (3) file encoding changed
entries.fix: Re-run the full pipeline to generate a fresh signed receipt. Ensure MLGG_HMAC_SECRET is set consistently across all pipeline stages. Do not edit attestation files manually.
entries.prevention: Store MLGG_HMAC_SECRET in a CI/CD secret, not in code or environment files. Treat execution receipts as write-once artifacts sealed at pipeline end.

entries.symptom: external_validation_gate fails minimum sample size requirement for the external cohort (EPV < 10 or n_events < 30)
entries.root_cause: External cohort has insufficient events/samples to reliably estimate performance metrics with acceptable CI width, making external validation statistically uninformative
entries.fix: Use a larger external cohort with at least 100 events (EPV >= 10 relative to predictor count). If no larger cohort is available, document this as a key limitation per TRIPOD+AI Item 21.
entries.prevention: Plan external validation cohort size during study design. Run sample_size_gate on the external cohort before external_validation_gate.

entries.symptom: fairness_equity_gate reports disparate impact ratio < 0.8 or equalized odds difference > 0.1 for a protected subgroup
entries.root_cause: Model exhibits differential performance across demographic subgroups (age, sex, ethnicity), potentially due to underrepresentation of the affected group in training data or features that proxy for protected attributes
entries.fix: Investigate which subgroup is disadvantaged. Apply fairness-aware training (reweighting, adversarial debiasing) or post-hoc threshold calibration per subgroup. Report subgroup-specific metrics per TRIPOD+AI Item 12 and Item 20.
entries.prevention: Ensure training data is balanced across protected subgroups. Audit features for proxy variables (e.g., zip code for race). Run fairness_equity_gate during model selection, not just at the end.

entries.symptom: feature_engineering_audit_gate fails because the feature engineering report is missing required fields (feature_count, transformation_log, or leakage_check_performed)
entries.root_cause: Feature engineering was performed without generating a structured audit report, or the report was generated by an older version of the pipeline that used a different schema
entries.fix: Re-run feature engineering with --generate-audit-report flag. Ensure feature_engineering_report.json includes all required fields per the schema in references/feature-engineering-report.example.json.
entries.prevention: Feature engineering scripts should always generate an audit report as a side effect. Add feature_engineering_audit_gate to pre-training CI checks.

entries.symptom: feature_lineage_gate detects a circular dependency in the feature derivation graph (feature A depends on feature B which depends on feature A)
entries.root_cause: Feature derivation logic creates a circular reference, typically caused by iterative feature construction that references the outcome or derived target values
entries.fix: Review feature_lineage.json for the circular path. Break the cycle by removing one of the circular dependencies. Ensure all derived features trace lineage to raw source variables only.
entries.prevention: Feature lineage tracking must be acyclic (DAG). Run feature_lineage_gate as part of feature engineering review, not just pre-training.

entries.symptom: generalization_gap_gate reports AUC_train - AUC_test > 0.1, indicating overfitting to the training set
entries.root_cause: Model has overfit to training data patterns, failing to generalize. Common causes: insufficient regularization, model complexity exceeds dataset size, or train/test distributions differ
entries.fix: Increase regularization (reduce max_depth, increase alpha/lambda, or add dropout). Reduce feature count via feature selection. Check for distribution shift with covariate_shift_gate.
entries.prevention: Use generalization_gap_gate as an early stopping criterion during model selection. A gap > 0.1 should trigger re-evaluation of model complexity.

entries.symptom: metric_consistency_gate flags an inconsistency: e.g., reported sensitivity does not match TP/(TP+FN) computed from the confusion matrix values in the same report
entries.root_cause: Metrics were computed independently at different thresholds or on different data subsets, leading to mathematically inconsistent values being reported together in the same evaluation report
entries.fix: Regenerate all metrics from the same confusion matrix at the same threshold. Use the confusion_matrix_to_metrics() utility to ensure all metrics are derived consistently from a single source.
entries.prevention: All threshold-dependent metrics must be derived from a single confusion matrix computation. Avoid computing sensitivity, specificity, PPV, NPV independently.

entries.symptom: missingness_policy_gate fails because Little's MCAR test rejects the null hypothesis (p < 0.05), indicating data is not Missing Completely At Random
entries.root_cause: Data has structured missingness patterns (MAR or MNAR), meaning simple imputation methods like mean/median imputation are invalid and will introduce bias
entries.fix: Use multiple imputation (MICE) for MAR data. For MNAR data, include a missingness indicator as a feature and document the MNAR mechanism in TRIPOD+AI Item 8.
entries.prevention: Always test missingness mechanism before choosing imputation strategy. Document the chosen imputation method and its justification per TRIPOD+AI Item 8.
entries.planned_fix: Add Little's MCAR test as optional check in missingness_policy_gate via --mcar-test flag

entries.symptom: reporting_bias_gate fails because one or more TRIPOD+AI or PROBAST+AI checklist items are not marked as completed in the checklist spec
entries.root_cause: The publication checklist (reporting-bias-checklist.example.json) has required items set to false or missing, indicating the manuscript does not satisfy the corresponding reporting standard requirement
entries.fix: For each failing item, refer to the tripod_ai_item_id in the gate failure report. Complete the corresponding section in the manuscript per the TRIPOD+AI 2024 guideline (Collins et al. BMJ 2024;385:e078378).
entries.prevention: Complete the reporting checklist in parallel with manuscript writing, not as a post-hoc exercise. Assign each TRIPOD+AI item to a manuscript section during the outline phase.

entries.symptom: request_contract_gate fails schema validation — required field missing or value type mismatch in request.json
entries.root_cause: The request.json configuration file does not conform to the expected schema (references/request-schema.example.json), either due to a missing required field, an invalid enum value, or a type mismatch
entries.fix: Validate request.json against the schema using: python3 scripts/schema_preflight.py --request request.json. Fix all reported violations before running the pipeline.
entries.prevention: Use mlgg.py onboarding to generate a valid request.json template. Run schema_preflight.py as part of the init step before any gate execution.

entries.symptom: robustness_gate reports performance variance across seeds (std_roc_auc) > threshold (typically 0.03), indicating the model is not robust to random initialization
entries.root_cause: Model performance is highly dependent on random seed, suggesting the model has overfit to a specific random initialization or the dataset is too small for stable learning
entries.fix: Use ensemble averaging across multiple seeds. Increase regularization. Verify dataset size is adequate with sample_size_gate. Report seed sensitivity in methods per TRIPOD+AI Item 9.
entries.prevention: Run robustness_gate as part of model selection. Set minimum training set size requirements that ensure stable convergence across random seeds.

entries.symptom: sample_size_gate fails because Events Per Variable (EPV) < 10, indicating the dataset is underpowered for the number of predictors
entries.root_cause: The outcome event count divided by the number of predictors is below the EPV threshold, meaning the model will likely be unstable and overfit in regression/hazard models
entries.fix: Reduce the predictor count to raise EPV above 10 (use feature selection). Alternatively, gather more data. EPV >= 20 is recommended per Riley et al. 2019 for stable prediction models.
entries.prevention: Run sample_size_gate before feature engineering finalization. Apply the rule: max_predictors = floor(n_events / 10) as a hard ceiling on feature count.

entries.symptom: seed_stability_gate reports max_auc_range > 0.05 across tested seeds, indicating critical seed sensitivity in model performance
entries.root_cause: Model AUC varies by more than 5 percentage points across random seeds, indicating instability in either the model learning process or the data split used for evaluation
entries.fix: Investigate whether instability is in the model or the split. Use seed_stability_gate --diagnose to partition variance. Consider switching to k-fold cross-validation for more stable performance estimates.
entries.prevention: Test seed stability on >=10 seeds before finalizing model selection. Report seed-averaged performance with SD as the primary performance metric.

agent_instructions.on_new_error: When encountering a new error not in this database: 1) Diagnose root cause 2) Apply fix 3) Verify fix works 4) Append new entry to this file with next sequential ID 5) Commit with message 'knowledge-base: add ERR-NNN <brief description>'
agent_instructions.on_known_error: When encountering a known error: 1) Look up the entry 2) Apply the documented fix 3) If fix doesn't work, update the entry with additional context 4) If the error has a new variant, add a sub-entry
agent_instructions.id_format: ERR-NNN where NNN is zero-padded sequential number
